meaning: adj.尚未找到的,未被发现的
examples: But no matter what its price, a work has no value unless it is seen; unfound masterpieces are worthless.
“The Cello Suites”, his first book, vividly chronicles his international search for the original, and unfound, Bach score.
"Crouching Tiger, Hidder Dragon" is not only the name of a movie, the phrase originally means unfound talented people.
Best of all, fears of hurricanes ruining cruises are mostly unfound since new ships are fast enough to steer clear of any danger and the worst that can happen is that your itinerary changes.
